title: Global Disease Burden and Epidemiological Transitions

learning_objectives.text: Define key concepts in global disease burden measurement including DALYs, QALYs, YLD, and YLL
Describe the epidemiological transition theory and its variations across different populations
Explain the relationship between demographic transitions and health patterns
Identify major data sources for global burden of disease estimates
Compare disease burden patterns across WHO regions and income levels
Interpret trends in global epidemiological transitions over the past century
Explain the concept of double and triple disease burdens in low- and middle-income countries
Discuss limitations and challenges in global disease burden measurement
Calculate basic disease burden measures using provided data
Apply epidemiological transition framework to analyze country-specific health patterns
Use GBD data to inform health priority setting exercises
Assess surveillance system performance using burden data quality indicators
Analyze factors driving epidemiological transitions in different global contexts
Evaluate the impact of globalization on disease pattern changes
Compare methodological approaches for disease burden estimation
Examine regional variations in epidemiological transition timing and patterns
Integrate demographic, epidemiological, and socioeconomic data to explain health transitions
Develop frameworks for predicting future disease burden trends
Synthesize evidence on climate change impacts on disease geography
Critically assess the validity and reliability of global burden estimates
Evaluate policy implications of different disease burden measurement approaches
Judge the effectiveness of surveillance systems in capturing disease burden changes

key_topics.topic: Global Burden of Disease Study methodology
Quality-adjusted life years (QALYs) and health utility

case_studies.title: Nigeria's Triple Disease Burden
case_studies.focus: Sub-Saharan African country managing communicable, non-communicable, and injury burdens

case_studies.title: Pacific Island Nations: Unique Disease Patterns
case_studies.focus: Small island developing states with distinctive epidemiological profiles

case_studies.title: Afghanistan: Post-Conflict Epidemiological Profile
case_studies.focus: Health pattern changes following conflict and reconstruction
